must_detect.matched_text:
Aurelia Grid Systems SE (ELE-listed issuer in the Union of Lydora) processes personal data to maintain insider lists, manage cyber incident response, and prepare CSRD/SFDR disclosures.
Legal bases: GDPR Art 6(1)(c),(f) and, where relevant, Art 9(2)(b),(g) for diversity or health data surfaced by whistleblowing or security event logs.
We process names, roles, private contact details, wallet hashes for pilot crypto-settlement testing, and network telemetry; insider-list entries include precise event timestamps and project codenames.
For EU MAR compliance, data linked to a non-public tokenisation pilot and an unannounced green PPA renegotiation are treated as inside information until public via ELE or our official channel.
Access is restricted by information barriers between the Digital Assets Lab and Treasury, with a blackout window imposed for “Project Larch” until the ELE announcement window opens.
Non-public commercial terms (discount rates on renewable certificates and service-level credits following a contained cyber incident) are classified as confidential and may be MNPI.
Cross-border transfers to our processors in Norlavia and Karska use SCCs (Module 2), with TIAs and encryption-in-use; occasional support access from a security operations vendor in Perennia is gated by EU SCCs and strict access logs.
Insider lists are retained for five years post-publication; cyber incident artefacts for eight years; CSRD/SFDR working papers for ten years; crypto wallet telemetry for 180 days, unless extended for regulatory requests.
Data subjects may request access, rectification, or restriction; where Art 23 restrictions apply during a live MAR investigation, responses may be deferred with reasons.
Internal carbon price assumptions for 2027 are non-public until the CSRD report is filed and are accessible only on a need-to-know basis.
Special category personal data will be minimised and pseudonymised; any Art 9 data in diversity metrics supporting CSRD will be aggregated before disclosure.
Employees are reminded that tipping or selective disclosure (including crypto token liquidity discussions) outside authorised channels is prohibited and monitored.
For operational readiness training, a marketing webinar may reference insider lists, cyber playbooks, and crypto wallet concepts purely for education, with no confidential or deal-stage information.
Data will not be transferred to third countries without appropriate safeguards and a documented transfer risk assessment; copies of SCCs may be requested subject to redactions.
Contact: Dr. Marius Petrov, Data Protection Officer, dpo@aureliagrid.eu; supervisory matters may be addressed to the competent authority of Lydora’s regulated market.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
